executive_summary: The Iran–US–Israel conflict has reached a critical inflection point following the US-Israeli coordinated strikes on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei and dozens of senior officials. Iran has launched massive retaliatory attacks—14+ US bases across Iraq, Syria, Gulf states, and Saudi Arabia—plus ballistic missile barrages on Israel and strikes on Dubai. The IRGC has announced 7th-8th waves of strikes. With 498 incidents in 24h (175 major), regional escalation is accelerating rapidly. The UK reports 300 personnel nearly hit in Bahrain; Iran's attacks on civilian targets in Israel have killed at least 4. International mediators are engaged but tensions are at unprecedented levels.
whats_new: IRGC announces 'seventh and eighth' wave of retaliatory missile strikes on US bases across Middle East (RIA Novosti)
Kuwaiti air defense intercepts hostile aerial targets in southern airspace (QNA)
UK reports approximately 300 British personnel within 200m of Iranian missile strike on US base in Bahrain (The Guardian, BBC, Daily Mail)
Iran fires missiles at Greek Cyprus hosting British troops—two missiles targeted (Daily Sabah)
Protests erupt in Pakistan and Iraq over Khamenei killing; US consulate in Karachi attacked with casualties (Daily Sabah, Al Alam)
Saudi and Kuwaiti defense ministers condemn Iranian attacks, indicating Gulf state alignment against Iran (QNA)
Egyptian President and Omani Sultan discuss regional military escalation (QNA)
Moscow condemns Khamenei's assassination as violation of morality and international law (The Hindu)
confirmed_events: US and Israel launched coordinated airstrikes on 20+ Iranian provinces, targeting government facilities and IRGC centers (multiple sources: RIA, Al Alam, Middle East Eye)
Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed in US-Israeli strike on Tehran compound (confirmed by: SCMP, Daily Sabah, Al Alam, The Hindu—multiple independent sources)
Iran launched ballistic missile barrage on Israel—targets Haifa, Galilee, Jerusalem area, and Beit Shemesh (confirmed by multiple sources)
Iran struck 14 US military bases across Iraq, Syria, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Gulf region (confirmed by multiple sources)
Iranian missile attacks on US base in Erbil, Iraq confirmed (multiple sources)
IDF destroyed two Iranian fighter jets (F4, F5) on runway before takeoff in Iran (Jerusalem Post)
US Navy support ship (MST) targeted and struck by IRGC missiles in Gulf region (multiple sources)
At least 60-85 children killed in school strike in southern Iran—attributed to US-Israeli airstrikes (multiple sources with rising casualty toll)
Explosions reported in Dubai—Iranian missile and suicide drone attack targeting Western civilians (confirmed by multiple sources including Daily Mail)
Oil tanker attacked off Oman coast near Duqm port—4 injured, crew evacuated (confirmed by The Hindu, RBC)
GCC condemns Iranian aggression against Duqm port and oil tanker (QNA)
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims 200 US military personnel killed in Iranian strikes on American bases—unverified casualty figure (Al Alam, single source)
IRGC claims mass casualties on US forces—specific numbers not independently confirmed
Death toll from Iran school strike rises to 85—specific attribution to US/Israel not independently verified
Iranian claims of destroying American radar in Qatar—specific damage assessment unverified
Four killed in Beit Shemesh rocket strike—attribution to specific Iranian proxy group varies

people_travel: Multiple international airlines suspending or rerouting flights through Gulf and Eastern Mediterranean airspace
CBSE postpones class 10, 12 board exams in West Asia region (The Hindu)
Dubai airport remains operational but under threat—Iranian drone attacks causing panic among travelers and influencers
Air defense intercepts active over Bahrain, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia—civilian flight paths affected
Tel Aviv and Jerusalem airports face ongoing rocket threats—flight disruptions expected to continue
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz under threat—closure fears causing crude oil price volatility globally (The Hindu)
Oil tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz (MKD Vyom, Marshall Islands-flagged)—commercial shipping in the Gulf of Oman at elevated risk
Russian shadow fleet tanker seized by Belgian special forces—adding to global oil trade disruptions
Duqm port in Oman attacked—Iranian aggression targeting critical Gulf logistics hub
GCC condemns Iranian attacks on Duqm commercial port—regional supply chain disruptions mounting
Power lines in Kuwait damaged by drone debris—potential infrastructure impacts
market_macro: Hong Kong government preparing for market volatility amid Iran-US conflict (SCMP)
Oil prices likely to spike further on Strait of Hormuz concerns—critical chokepoint under threat
Global energy markets on high alert as conflict expands across Gulf producing states
Death of Khamenei creating uncertainty in oil markets—Iran's future output andexport policy unclear
Regional stock markets facing significant pressure—Dubai, Riyadh, Kuwait exchanges volatile

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is on an escalatory trajectory with no immediate de-escalation signals. Iran has lost its Supreme Leader and will likely pursue vengeance through proxies and direct strikes. The IRGC's announcement of 7th-8th wave attacks indicates sustained campaign. Expect: (1) continued Iranian missile/drone attacks on US bases and Israeli cities; (2) Israeli retaliation against Iranian infrastructure; (3) increased attacks on commercial shipping in Gulf/Arabian Sea; (4) regional Gulf states likely to side with US/Israel diplomatically and potentially militarily. The 24h window shows 498 incidents with 175 major—the conflict intensity is stable but at extremely high levels.
escalation_risks: Iranian attacks on Gulf state infrastructure—Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE now directly targeted
Risk of UK/coalition military involvement—British forces directly threatened in Bahrain
Potential Iranian attacks on Turkish territory or Cyprus—missiles already fired at Greek Cyprus
Hezbollah and Iraqi proxy escalation—increasing attacks on Israel
Risk of accidental NATO Article 5 trigger if Gulf state territory attacked with coalition casualties
Civilian casualty escalation could provoke stronger Iranian response or internal political pressure
Oil supply disruption if Strait of Hormuz closed or targeted
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears highly unlikely in the near term given the elimination of Iran's Supreme Leader—creating a massive leadership vacuum and political imperative for retaliation. Possible pathways: (1) major power mediation (Russia, China) pushing for ceasefire to prevent regional collapse; (2) complete Iranian military incapacitation preventing further strikes; (3) US-Israel decision to declare mission accomplished and accept negotiated end; (4) internal Iranian political fracture creating leadership transition crisis. However, with IRGC conducting waves of sustained attacks and Tehran vowing 'heavy price,' the conflict is likely to intensify before any negotiated settlement becomes feasible. International community increasingly divided—with Russia condemning the assassination while UK and Australia support US actions.
